page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288 is a high-capacity instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 288 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 144 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The memory configuration provides 2232 GB of RAM, translating to 7.75 GB per vCPU. The processor includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 312050.11 and a Passmark CPU score of 176858.39. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with read-only cached small-block bandwidth reaching 23049.05 GB/sec and uncached large-block write bandwidth at 412.0 GB/sec. Single-threaded performance is also strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2302.0 and a gzip compression speed of 36.58 GB/sec. For database operations, the server processed 405.42 million Redis SET operations per second.

This instance is optimized for memory-intensive and highly parallelized compute workloads, such as large-scale database operations, software compilation, and memory-bound data processing. While it excels in CPU-based tasks and prompt processing for large language models, achieving 39.04 tokens/sec on a 70B model, it exhibits weaker performance in text generation for smaller models. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a key tradeoff, making it less suitable for tasks requiring direct-attached storage speed or hardware-accelerated graphics and deep learning training.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 288 dedicated vCPUs and 2232 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. Designed for large-scale enterprise workloads, it delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, database operations, and memory bandwidth, as demonstrated by a Passmark CPU score of 176858.39 and 405.42 million Redis SET operations per second. The processor includes 504 MB of L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions. However, the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, presenting a tradeoff for storage-heavy or graphics-intensive tasks. It is highly suited for memory-bound applications, large databases, software compilation, and parallelized CPU-based data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288 is a memory-optimized instance with 288 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2232 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288 features 288 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2232 GB RAM, optimized for high-performance database and memory-bound workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, memory-optimized instance delivering exceptional multi-core compute and massive memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise database and parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors.
2232 GB of RAM supporting high-bandwidth memory operations.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for advanced vector and matrix calculations.
Top-tier multi-core performance and Redis database throughput.
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware.
Best suited for large databases, software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
